sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, date_col DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2023-01-01'), (2, DATE '2023-12-31'), (3, DATE '-infinity'), (4, NULL);
SELECT id, date_col, isinf(date_col) AS is_infinite FROM test_dates WHERE date_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, date_col + INTERVAL (d.days) DAY AS extended_date FROM (SELECT days FROM (VALUES (7), (30)) AS d(days)) AS d GROUP BY id, date_col;
SELECT id, date_col, isinf(date_col + INTERVAL (d.days) DAY) AS is_extended_infinite FROM test_dates, (VALUES (10), (20)) AS d(days) WHERE date_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, date_col, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY isinf(date_col)) AS infinite_count FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, date_col + INTERVAL (d.days) DAY AS result_date FROM test_dates AS t JOIN (SELECT days FROM (VALUES (1), (2), (3), (4), (5), (10)) AS d(days)) ON t.id = d.days WHERE d.days IS NOT NULL;

SELECT t.id, isinf(d.date_col + INTERVAL (1 - d.items) DAY) AS is_date_infinite FROM (SELECT id, date_col FROM test_dates) AS t, (SELECT 1 AS items, CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL (1) DAY AS date_col) AS d WHERE d.date_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t.id, isinf(date_col + INTERVAL 10 DAY) AS is_future_infinite FROM test_dates AS t WHERE date_col IS NOT NULL OR date_col IS NULL;
SELECT id, isinf(DATE '-infinity') AS infinite_status FROM (VALUES (1), (2), (3)) AS t(id) WHERE infinite_status = true;
